task.title: Cleaning the Print Head Path
task.shortdesc: If the print quality has not improved after cleaning and aligning the print head and

context.p: If the print quality has not improved after cleaning and aligning the print head and cleaning the paper path, the print head path inside the printer may be smeared with ink.
To clean the print head path, use the following:

ul.li: ¼ cup (59 ml) water containing 2 to 3 drops of mild dish detergent

context.note: Do not use any other liquid to clean the print head path or you may damage your product. Do not use tissue paper to clean the inside of the product. The print head nozzles may become clogged with lint.

step.cmd: Close the document cover, if necessary.
Lift up the scanner unit with both hands until it locks.

info.note: Do not touch the flat white cable, translucent film, or ink tubes inside the product. Doing so may cause a malfunction.

step.cmd: Using the flashlight, check the translucent film shown here for ink smears.

info.note: Do not touch the print head rail or wipe the grease off of it. You may not be able to print.

step.cmd: Moisten a cotton swab with the water and detergent mixture, and squeeze out any excess moisture. Lightly wipe the ink off the translucent film.

info.note: Do not press too hard on the film or you may dislocate the anchor springs and damage your product.

step.cmd: Use a new, dry cotton swab to dry the translucent film.
Repeat steps 7 and 8 as necessary to clean all ink smears.
When the translucent film is dry, close the scanner unit.

info.note: The scanner unit is designed to close slowly with slight resistance to help prevent injury to your fingers. Continue to close the scanner unit even if you feel some resistance.

step.cmd: Reconnect any disconnected cables, plug in the power cord, and turn on the product.
